dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3488 IS: Asylum Reform and Loophole Closure Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Cotton introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend the Immigration and Nationality Act to modify eligibility for asylum, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Asylum Reform and Loophole Closure Act.

subsection.text: Section 208 of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1158) is amended—

clause.text: by striking if the Attorney General determines that and inserting if the Attorney General or the Secretary of Homeland Security determines that—;
by striking the alien may be and inserting the following:
by inserting or the Secretary of Homeland Security before finds that;
by striking the period at the end and inserting ; or; and
adding at the end the following:

the alien entered, attempted to enter, or arrived in the United States after transiting through at least one country outside the alien's country of nationality (or, in the case of an alien having no nationality, the country of the alien's last habitual residence) en route to the United States, unless the alien demonstrates that he or she applied for protection from persecution or torture in each such country through which the alien so transited, and the alien received a final judgement denying the alien protection in such country.

subparagraph.text: by redesignating subparagraphs (C) and (E) as subparagraphs (B) and (C), respectively;
in subparagraph (B), as redesignated, by striking Subject to subparagraph (D), paragraph (1) and inserting Paragraph (1); and
in subparagraph (C), as redesignated, by striking Subparagraphs (A) and (B) and inserting Subparagraph (A).

subsection.header: Permanent ineligibility for illegal entry or visa fraud
subsection.text: Section 208(b)(2)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1158(b)(2)(A)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in clause (v), by striking ; or and inserting a semicolon;
in clause (vi), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

subclause.text: has committed, or attempted to commit, unlawful entry in violation of section 275; or

has attempted to enter or has obtained entry to the United States by a willfully false or misleading representation or the willful concealment of a material fact, including by attempting to obtain or obtaining a fraudulent visa.

subsection.header: Higher standard for establishing credible fear of persecution
subsection.text: Section 235(b)(1)(B)(v) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1225(b)(1)(B)(v)) is amended by striking there is a significant possibility and inserting it is more likely than not.

During expedited removal or asylum proceedings under section 235 of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1225), a child may be detained together with his or her parent for a period of not more than 180 days.
